milestone_brief:
Close UPL-4 (adopt qtawesome icons) from the 2026q2-graph-and-window frontend-uplift final report. RICE 8.0, ranked #6 overall, challenger-MINOR. 3-brief triangulation (library scout's top icon candidate + inspiration via PC-3/PC-11 implicit + visual scout's M-3 Reset Camera button styling). Source: .claude/notes/frontend-uplifts/2026q2-graph-and-window/artifacts/final-report.md UPL-4 + challenger MINOR.

V0 scope per challenger: ship the 3 highest-value targets — Reset Camera (View panel), Screenshot (View panel), Reset Defaults (Parameters panel). The 5 camera-preset buttons (+X, -X, +Y, -Y, +Z, -Z, Isometric) + Wireframe/Show-edges toggles defer to v1 (separate follow-up milestone) so this stays a discrete S-effort milestone.

Track choice: lazy-import qtawesome via a new module-level helper (suggested file: `icons.py` at repo root, per challenger lesson). The cold-boot cost of qtawesome's icon-font cache is ~150-200ms — lazy-import the qtawesome module ITSELF (not just per-icon functions) so the cost fires on first icon use rather than at app launch.

Scope:

1. requirements.txt — Add `qtawesome>=1.4.2` (MIT, 930 stars, PySide6 6.8.x segfaults fixed in v1.4.1 January 2026 per the library scout brief).

2. icons.py — New module at repo root. Single source of truth for icon construction. Pattern:
- `def reset_camera_icon(theme="dark") -> QIcon`
- `def screenshot_icon(theme="dark") -> QIcon`
- `def reset_defaults_icon(theme="dark") -> QIcon`
Each function lazy-imports qtawesome on first call (`global _qta; if _qta is None: import qtawesome as _qta`), resolves color from styles.py per the active theme (use PALETTE_DARK['TEXT_VALUE'] for dark, PALETTE_LIGHT['TEXT_VALUE'] for light — the icon color should match the active panel text color). Recommended icon names: mdi6.video-marker-outline or mdi6.camera-control for Reset Camera; mdi6.camera or fa6s.camera for Screenshot; mdi6.restore or fa6s.rotate-left for Reset Defaults. Researcher: pick exact names from the qtawesome 1.4.2 icon set and verify they exist.

3. view_panel.py — Apply icons to the Reset Camera button (currently `objectName("resetCameraBtn")`) and Screenshot button via `button.setIcon(icons.reset_camera_icon())` etc. The button SIZE may need a small bump if icon + text doesn't fit; verify visually. Default theme is dark (per dark-mode-2026q2-e1) so icons.*_icon() default arg = "dark" is correct for launch state.

4. parameters_panel.py — Apply icon to the "Reset all to defaults" button (`self._reset_btn`, objectName resetDefaultsBtn). Same pattern as #3.

5. app.py — When theme changes via _on_theme_changed/_apply_system_theme, the icons need re-construction with the new color. Either:
(a) re-create icons by calling icons.*_icon(active_theme) and re-setIcon on each button — clean but the panels don't know about themes
(b) add a `refresh_icons(active_theme)` method on each panel that the MainWindow calls
(c) use qtawesome's IconWidget pattern (its `qta.icon()` returns a re-rendered icon each call; cheap to swap)
Researcher: pick the cleanest pattern that doesn't tangle panels into theme awareness.

6. tests/ — Add test_icons.py: smoke-test each `icons.*_icon()` function returns a valid QIcon (not isNull()) for both "light" and "dark" themes. Test must be Qt-free where possible OR use the same lightweight pattern as test_set_default_color (no QApplication needed for QIcon construction if qtawesome supports it offscreen — researcher to verify).

7. CONTEXT.md — Brief mention in §3 (dep stack rationale) that qtawesome is the icon source + lazy-imported on first use.

AI invariants:
- AI-1: qtawesome is MIT, PySide6-compatible (v1.4.1 fixed Qt6 segfaults). Adds a single small lib; not an alternative renderer.
- AI-2: Qt-free tests preferred; QIcon construction may need QApplication — researcher to verify and document. If QApplication required, mark the test xfail under offscreen or use mock.
- AI-9: icon construction is synchronous; no processEvents.
- AI-11: qualified Qt enums for any new QIcon / QAction usage.
- AI-12: icon color must use TEXT_VALUE from the active palette (5.83-11.6:1 vs panel bg); no raw hex.
- AI-13: 6-digit hex when passing color to qta.icon(color=...).

V0 cut explicit:
- 3 buttons get icons (Reset Camera, Screenshot, Reset Defaults)
- The 7 camera-preset buttons (+X, -X, +Y, -Y, +Z, -Z, Isometric) — deferred to a v1 milestone (qtawesome-icons-2026q2-e2 or similar)
- Wireframe / Show-edges toggle icons — deferred to v1
- Spinner icon during render (qta.IconWidget with rotation) — deferred (would couple to AI-9 render path)
- No menu-bar icons — deferred

Validation:
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q passes
- Manual: run app.py and verify the 3 buttons show icons matching the active theme; toggle Theme menu and verify icons re-render with the new color
- Cold-boot timing: app.py launch time should not regress by more than 50ms (lazy import means qtawesome only loads on first button paint, not at module import)
research_synthesis: Sonnet researcher picked mdi6.camera-retake/mdi6.camera/mdi6.restore for the 3 buttons. icons.py module with per-function lazy import (_qta=None sentinel) + theme-aware _icon_color(theme) via PALETTE_*['TEXT_VALUE']. Pattern A refresh_icons(theme) method on ViewPanel + ParametersPanel, called from MainWindow.__init__ AND from _on_theme_changed AND _apply_system_theme — critical because qta.icon() requires a running QApplication (silently returns empty QIcon otherwise). Researcher caught R1+R3: shot_btn and reset_btn in view_panel.py are local variables; must be promoted to instance attrs. 4-test plan: 3 AI-2-compliant pure-Python (lazy-import deferral, color routing, mock qta args) + 1 QApplication-guarded (non-isNull). qtawesome>=1.4.2,<2 MIT. ~148 LOC across 7 files — inline.
